attacking_style: Opportunistic breakaway specialist who targets intermediate stages rather than mountain-top finishes. Excels in winning from small groups (7 riders) when team obligations are minimal, particularly when freed from domestique duties.
team_tactics: Performs best when team protection is absent or GC leadership compromised, suggesting he thrives with tactical freedom rather than structured team support. Less effective in protected sprinter role.
terrain_preference: Stages suited to breakaways that survive to finish, particularly those where coalition dynamics delay peloton chase until final 8km. Avoids pure mountain stages, but competitive in ITTs despite not specializing.
vulnerability: Constrained by team obligations when required to work for protected riders. Performance depends heavily on tactical freedom and team willingness to let him hunt stages opportunistically.
key_patterns: Multiple stage wins from breakaways when given freedom (3 Vuelta wins, 2021)
Near-miss ITT performance shows sustained form deep into Grand Tours (fourth place final ITT)
Coalition breakaway tactics successful: 7-rider group holding 18-second margin demonstrates timing intelligence
form_trajectory: positive: Three stage victories plus strong ITT finish late in Vuelta demonstrates maintained form throughout three-week race rather than early-race depletion
built_from: 2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 19
2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 19
